context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=20; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected no microwave for fruit meals, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature. IF the meal is a warm savory item in a social context, including with a person on the left or right, THEN 1 min or 2 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE observed social savory meals support moderate reheating rather than hot extremes. IF the meal is a warm savory item while watching TV on the left THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a shorter reheat in that left-TV context. IF the meal is a warm savory item while watching TV on the right THEN 3 min BECAUSE the new right-TV savory meal shows a preference for hotter food in that context. IF the meal is a warm savory item while working on a laptop on the left or in front THEN 1 min or 3 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both shorter and longer reheating in work contexts, indicating meal-specific variation rather than one fixed work-context value.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization in front-social, front-TV, and front-laptop contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity to the right of the user THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected right-occlusion minimization in right-oriented contexts, and the new right-TV meal strengthens this pattern beyond social settings alone. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user wants the robot to avoid blocking a person on that side. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN minimize left occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in left-TV meals, showing left-side sensitivity that is present but not fixed. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected no occlusion handling in observed left-laptop work meals, so left-side work alone has not triggered occlusion avoidance.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves a social partner to the right THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow in repeated right-social meals, indicating a cautious pace in that setting. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN slow BECAUSE the new right-TV meal shows the user also prefers a cautious pace in that right-oriented viewing context. IF dining context involves a social partner to the left THEN medium BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows a moderate pace preference in that social setting. IF dining context involves a social partner or valued activity directly in front of the user THEN medium or fast depending on the meal/context BECAUSE the user selected medium in front-oriented TV and laptop contexts and has also selected fast while working on a laptop in front, indicating a tendency toward quicker motion when attention is front-focused. IF dining context involves working on a laptop to the right THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium in that right-work context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN fast BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected fast in that left-TV context, suggesting a preference to reduce interruption duration there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN slow or medium depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both values in left-laptop work meals, indicating a cautious but not fixed pace there.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food acquisition must remain compatible with limited mouth opening in general THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user's physical profile supports narrower bites and this remains the dominant observed choice, including the new chicken-nugget meal. IF the food is a bite-acquisition-challenging savory item such as chicken, fish bites, or similar solid pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis or parallel to major axis depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has sometimes selected the higher-acquisition-success option for solid savory meals but has also selected parallel for at least one chicken-based meal, indicating that limited mouth opening remains an important competing consideration.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF the user's physical constraints are relevant to the meal context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and observed selections consistently favor the mode that does not require leaning forward.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside mouth transfer. IF the user's physical constraints are considered for food delivery THEN not applicable BECAUSE the observed preference and physical profile support inside mouth transfer rather than outside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front or to the right THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in both front- and right-laptop work contexts, consistent with a low-disruption visual cue during work. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN LED or speech + LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both in left-laptop work meals, indicating a preference for visual signaling there with occasional desire for added salience. IF dining context involves a social partner to the left or right THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in observed left-social and right-social meals, suggesting visual-only cues are acceptable in social side contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in front-TV meals, aligning with the broader preference for unobtrusive visual signaling when attention is directed forward. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN speech or LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both cue modalities in that context, so a non-combined single cue appears preferred there without a stable winner. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new right-TV meal shows the user preferred a more salient multimodal cue in that setting.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF initiating feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts, consistent with maintaining explicit control while avoiding unreliable mouth-based interaction. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left or right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in observed social meals on both sides, reinforcing that social settings do not override the preference for explicit manual signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context, indicating that left-TV settings can shift toward lower-effort automation but do not consistently override explicit control. IF a context would require choosing between open mouth interaction and button input THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction less suitable for this user.

IF initiating drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts, consistent with maintaining explicit control while avoiding unreliable mouth-based interaction. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left or right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in observed social meals on both sides, reinforcing that social settings do not override the preference for explicit manual signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context, indicating that left-TV settings can shift toward lower-effort automation but do not consistently override explicit control. IF a context would require choosing between open mouth interaction and button input THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction less suitable for this user.

IF initiating mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts, consistent with maintaining explicit control while avoiding unreliable mouth-based interaction. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left or right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in observed social meals on both sides, reinforcing that social settings do not override the preference for explicit manual signaling. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context, indicating that left-TV settings can shift toward lower-effort automation but do not consistently override explicit control. IF a context would require choosing between open mouth interaction and button input THEN button BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction less suitable for this user.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front or to the right THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in both front- and right-laptop work contexts, consistent with a low-disruption visual cue during work. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN LED or speech + LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both in left-laptop work meals, indicating a preference for visual signaling there with occasional desire for added salience. IF dining context involves a social partner to the left or right THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in observed left-social and right-social meals, suggesting visual-only cues are acceptable in social side contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in front-TV meals, aligning with the broader preference for unobtrusive visual signaling when attention is directed forward. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN speech or LED depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both cue modalities in that context, so a non-combined single cue appears preferred there without a stable winner. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new right-TV meal shows the user preferred a more salient multimodal cue in that setting.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF completing feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected the reliable perception-based option across observed contexts, including work, social, and TV meals, and this avoids requiring extra physical input.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF completing drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based drinking completion less suitable. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts, consistent with explicit completion signaling that does not rely on head gestures. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left or right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in observed social meals on both sides, reinforcing that social context does not shift this user toward head-gesture perception. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context, indicating that left-TV settings can sometimes favor lower-effort automatic progression but not consistently. IF a context would require choosing between perception and button for drinking completion THEN button BECAUSE limited neck control makes head-gesture-based perception less suitable.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF completing mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based wiping completion less suitable. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button across observed work contexts, consistent with explicit completion signaling that does not rely on head gestures. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left or right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in observed social meals on both sides, reinforcing that social context does not shift this user toward head-gesture perception. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN button BECAUSE the user selected button in those TV contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN button or autocontinue depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both options in that context, indicating that left-TV settings can sometimes favor lower-effort automatic progression but not consistently. IF a context would require choosing between perception and button for wiping completion THEN button BECAUSE limited neck control makes head-gesture-based perception less suitable.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction in front-oriented contexts, including front-TV and front-laptop meals, consistent with wanting to reduce prolonged visual obstruction of the main focus. IF dining context includes a social partner to the left or right THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction in observed social meals on both sides, so social presence favors reducing obstruction. IF dining context involves a focal activity to the right without a social partner THEN no BECAUSE the user selected no retraction while working on a laptop to the right and also selected no retraction in the new right-TV meal, suggesting that right-sided visual activities alone do not trigger a preference for added retraction time. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN no BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected no retraction in that left-TV context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN no BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected no retraction in left-laptop work meals, matching the broader tendency to skip retraction for side-oriented non-social activities.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social THEN less or do not dip depending on the meal BECAUSE the user consistently avoids heavy dipping in social settings, with social sauce-containing meals showing reduced or suppressed dipping to limit messiness. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves a front-focused activity such as working on a laptop or watching TV in front THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected less dipping for sauce-containing meals in front-oriented contexts, indicating a stable tendency to reduce messiness when attention is directed to a valued front activity. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left THEN less BECAUSE the user selected less dipping in a left-laptop meal with sauce, showing that left-work contexts can also favor reduced dipping when sauce is present. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV on the right THEN less BECAUSE the new right-TV meal with ketchup shows the user prefers reduced dipping rather than no dipping or heavy dipping in that context. IF food does not include a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant and the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals. IF the meal has sauce already on the food but no separate dipping action is desired, including in left-TV contexts, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for sauced savory meals in that context.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, on the left, or to the right THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait across observed work contexts, consistent with preserving ample time to intervene while engaged in a work task. IF dining context involves a social partner on the left or right THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait in observed social meals on both sides, indicating a preference for ample intervention time in social settings. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or on the right THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longest wait in those TV contexts, indicating that these attention-demanding settings also favor preserving intervention time. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected the shortest wait in that context, indicating a stable preference for quick automatic progression if the system is waiting.
